data_contracts_note: File content reload is mentioned but no specifics on what state is preserved/discarded, how Document object mutation works, or what diagnostic format is expected

CONTAINS: Root cause: LSP server internal document state becomes out of sync with disk when multiple VSCode instances edit the same file
Specific scenario triggering bug: nested workspaces opening same file via different paths
Concrete symptom: diagnostics at wrong positions from stale file content
Solution approach: implement textDocument/didSave handler to reload from disk
Specific implementation detail: use FileContent::FromServer for fresh read
Test plan with step-by-step reproduction (6 steps)
Scope boundary: internal LSP handler fix, no docs needed
Affected file type example: JSON with syntax errors
MISSING: What happens if file reload fails or times out? Fallback behavior?
Does this handle concurrent edits during the reload? Race condition considerations?
What diagnostic information is cleared/rebuilt? Full redraw or incremental update?
Does this affect performance for large files? Any thresholds?
What state in Document object gets mutated? All fields or specific ones?
Is there a backup of old diagnostic state if reload needs to be reverted?
How are other open instances notified? Is there cross-instance synchronization?
Does this work for all file types or just JSON? Is it language-agnostic?
What happens if disk file was deleted between opens? Or file permissions changed?
Expected diagnostic output format—does it validate against schema?
Specific file paths/module names being changed (only handler name mentioned)
Quantifiable success metric beyond 'correct diagnostics' (e.g., latency, no errors in logs)
Rollback/regression risk—what existing behavior might break?
Whether internal caching layers need invalidation
